centroid_definition.coordinate_requirement: Each local I must be represented as a vector in a common G15-coordinate embedding before the centroid is computed.
centroid_definition.definition_candidate: Given a finite local-I register embedded in G15-coordinate space, the G15 centroid is the arithmetic mean of the embedded local-I coordinate vectors over the selected normalization domain.
centroid_definition.lawful_role: The centroid does not replace I. It lets every local I report drift in a shared G15 frame.
centroid_definition.name: G15 centroid for local-I register
centroid_definition.normalization_domain_default: all local-I candidate points in the slot/C register unless a smaller admitted domain is explicitly declared

keeper_line: The centroid does not replace I. It lets every I tell the truth about its drift.

local_i_definition.definition_candidate: A local receipt center or forward socket assigned a coordinate vector in the G15 quotient frame.
local_i_definition.name: local I point

normalization_axioms.statement: Centroid normalization is only defined after all local I points share one coordinate space.
Subtracting the G15 centroid changes the origin of comparison but not the internal differences among local I points.
The centroid does not select a receipt. It normalizes already declared local I candidates.
Observed, proxy-invalid, and gate-absent statuses are carried through normalization unless a separate theorem proves a status transition.

theorem_relevance.why_next: The centroid is only formal after local I points are embedded as vectors in G15 coordinates.

unresolved_requirements.requirement: Define actual G15 coordinate vectors for the slot/C local I points.
Decide whether centroid uses all 6561 candidates, only source-packet candidates, or another explicitly admitted domain.
Declare what comparisons after normalization are allowed: affine, metric, quotient, or status-only.
